podcasts.title: 269.真人短剧的下一战：与AI共生、工业化和好故事
podcasts.summary:
🎙【本期嘉宾】侯超、李佳佳
侯超（日新阅益文化董事长）
李佳佳（刚刚好影视董事长）
主播：潘乱（「乱翻书」主理人）
🎬 本期视频播客同步上线于@潘乱的视频号、哔哩哔哩、微博、小红书、Youtube等平台。
⏰【时间线】
一、生产方式的分化
02:46
为什么西安、郑州成了短剧之都？
05:03
一个月200多部，产能天花板在哪，怎么管理？
07:00
钱、本子、导演、编剧、演员从哪来？
08:07
「第一是本金安全，第二是利润规模」
08:33
短剧从付费转免费，创作团队，公司流程有哪些变化？
11:32
反脸谱化，人物关系做扎实
13:57
从男频到女频，从付费到免费，用户变了什么？
14:50
剧本决定一切，现在也依然是这样
15:36
25万一部和80万一部，两套打法
17:17
AI冲击，真人剧差点停掉
17:48
降本增效，哪些钱能省，哪些不能省？
18:00
「一切观众看不到的，感受不到的，都要省掉。感动自我的钱就不应该花。」
19:00
语言体系，导演和编剧的对齐
二、内容生产机制与好故事
22:17
选题从哪来？
27:52
套路与反套路
31:01
绕了一圈，又回到“用心做内容”
31:39
「人物关系是基石。底子打不实，上面再动没有用」
33:21
为什么创业？
35:48
「谁不想做出好作品？谁不想精心打磨，做一个大家都又叫好又叫座的作品呢？」
36:23
「卷剧本、卷体系都对，卷成本是卷错了方向」
37:37
编剧中心制在短剧里成立吗？
39:59
「最好的导演、最好的编剧、最好的演员，加起来不是最好的作品。因为不知道该听谁的」
41:17
演员从哪来？看重的是知名度、脸熟、表演、适配度，还是稳定性？
42:00
「演戏的内核，就是最适合这个角色的演员，去演好这个角色。」
44:25
「ROI当大于1的时候，我就应该多拍，它不是可观的，但它是可控的」
44:53
怎么提升爆款率？
47:51
项目失败了，复盘看什么？
三、AI变量+未来展望
51:31
AI和真人，已经是两条不同的赛道了
51:51
哪些选题适合AI，哪些选题适合真人？
55:39
AI在短剧工厂里的另一种用法
57:18
「我们只要跑过斩杀线就行」
57:26
「他保下限，我冲上限」
58:52
AI导演满地都是的时代，什么样的短剧，在未来还能被记住？
61:51
互动影游，要不要跟？
63:22
「不当第一个吃螃蟹的人，可能这条路本身就走不通」
66:08
短剧能不能成为一个艺术门类？
70:06
「它只要真的好，它才成艺术」
🎵【开场&结尾音乐】
开场&结尾音乐：Mount Dreams - Wolfs
ℹ【关于「乱翻书」】
「乱翻书」是一档关注商业、科技和互联网的圆桌对话节目。关心How和Why，以及少有人注意到的What。内容主要方向是科技考古、行业观察和前沿思考，研究公司的兴衰循环，希望能够为你带来信息增量。
「乱翻书」主理人是潘乱，代表作品有《
腾讯没有梦想
》、字节跳动/快手早期关键节点的系列特写。
【图】▲录制合影（左起：李佳佳、侯超、潘乱）
本期编辑：怀杭

podcasts.title: How a Writer Uses AI Without Losing His Voice

tweets.text:
one detail i enjoyed about @theo's keynote is in a world of claudeslopped thin color border "professional" looking slides, his went for the hand drawn @excalidraw /@tldraw look which immediately conveys humanity, thoughtfulness, parsimonious concision over badly rewarded verbosity.

fable could never

most agent labs are shy about acknowledging chinese model use because they need to sell to gov/defense

cog team did the hard part to productionize:
1. build a multilingual propaganda & censorship eval 
2. successfully correct for it in posttraining
3. serve at 1000 tok/s cheaply https://t.co/Av85Bh4zUf https://t.co/w59YVAWtTr
What's something that you're surprised @GeminiApp can't do well, and we should have fixed a long time ago?
This is pretty epic https://t.co/ktxH05b32i
Here's what happened when I ran /checkup https://t.co/W6thoLW6rm

New in Claude Code: /checkup

Run /checkup to:

1. Clean up unused skills/MCPs/plugins and save context
2. Dedup your local CLAUDE.md against the checked in CLAUDE.md
3. Break up root CLAUDE.md into nested CLAUDE.md's + skills
4. Turn off slow hooks
5. Update your Claude Code to the latest version
6. Enable auto mode by default
7. Pre-approve frequently denied read-only commands

.. And a few other goodies.

/checkup confirms with you before making any changes. Enjoy!

You know OpenAI is cooking when the sushi and tacos orders pile at the entrance of the office at 11pm. 

Bizarrely we need to eat to cook.
We were resetting last week. This week we are SHIPPING. Tune in for the livestream at 10am. https://t.co/ycWcTI3xSD

I can finally talk about this.

I've been playing with GTA 6 for about a month now.

It's pretty great, arguably even better than GTA 5.

Important Product Marketing lesson! You’re writing a story to transmit to your audience, but also for your audience to transmit to their audience. 

PMM > Sales > Customer
PMM > Customer > Peer
PMM > User > Buyer
PMM > Champion > Org https://t.co/cVsidZKJcO

I watched Glenngarry Glenn Ross again recently, and the Alec Baldwin bit that struck me wasn’t “always be closing” — 

it was [First place gets a car, second place gets steak knives, third place is fired]

Power law in action!

Tomorrow at 10am PT I'm hosting a live walkthrough of how we progressed from single-player Claude Code to multi-player Claude Tag. Then, we're going deep on how Claude Tag actually works.

AI used to finish your sentence. Then, it wrote entire features. Now, Claude Tag can monitor your channels, do proactive work for you, the whole team can steer it, and it remembers what you told it last week.

Register: https://t.co/D8Lz61BXn4

this should be a huge update in your model of software engineering: rewrites can be good, cheap and fast

of course most apps are not as testable and verifiable as Bun, but the models will continue to get better at filling those gaps https://t.co/LjygnPM31O
When do we stop comparing autonomous agents to hand-written code? You don’t see compilers comparing to engineers hand-writing assembly. https://t.co/YQBIPn8Eux
Should we add CAD 3D modeling to Replit? https://t.co/5NsKXGDuxw

AI will make all software Native.

Uncompromising performance and platform affinity. https://t.co/aYQ7ViNnDJ
Grok 4.5 now available to all Vercel customers https://t.co/AwObgo6KRu
The blissful sound of all the pieces of the agent stack clicking together. ht @nishimiya. This will power my personal productivity agents! https://t.co/BRenj3pXog

The latest AI models being dropped are getting insanely good handling complex knowledge worker tasks, and especially dealing with sophisticated domains of work like legal, professional services, healthcare, and more. Grok 4.5 is another great entry here, especially on cost + performance. 

As models get much better at coding, math, reasoning, and are trained on a variety of key verticals, we’re going to see more leaps in what you can do with enterprise data and documents.

the start of a new era

try Grok 4.5 in Cursor and let us know how it feels! https://t.co/GMQiu1CQ3Q
